issues.display_title: Issue #1459: Publishing stuck: update_dates unserialize crashes RabbitMQ publishing worker (ConstellationClass)
issues.main_description_html:
Description

New tours stopped publishing. Reported for user_id = 46677 — the user published 711 tours this month, but new tours are no longer publishing, and the tours are not marked to be published.

Root cause (already diagnosed)

There was an issue with update_dates unserialization that was crashing the publishing RabbitMQ worker. When update_dates failed to unserialize into an array, the worker crashed and publishing stalled.

Location: app/Class/ConstellationClass.php (around line 807)

Fix (applied locally, needs to land in git):

$aUpdates = @unserialize(str_replace('%22', '"', $auditId['update_dates']));
if (!is_array($aUpdates)) {
    $aUpdates = [];
}

From the report: "I've fixed the problem with publishing — there was an issue with update-date unserialize that was crashing the publishing rabbitmq worker. Basically you need to update the version from git for /home/jenkins/workspace/gothru-api-live/app/Class/ConstellationClass.php:807."

"I fixed it, but you need to apply the fix in GitHub if that is in git."

Action required

  • Apply / confirm the update_dates unserialize guard fix in app/Class/ConstellationClass.php in the git repo (the live server was hotfixed directly and must be reconciled with git so the next deploy doesn't overwrite it).
  • Verify the publishing RabbitMQ worker no longer crashes on malformed/non-array update_dates.
  • Confirm publishing resumes for user_id 46677 (try publishing the top 2–3 tours) and that tours get correctly marked as published.

Acceptance Criteria

  1. The update_dates unserialize guard is committed in git (ConstellationClass.php) so it survives deploys.
  2. The publishing worker processes jobs without crashing when update_dates is malformed or not an array.
  3. New tours for the affected user publish successfully and are marked as published.

Demo from Developer

issues.display_title: Issue #4103: Allow Basic Accounts to Edit Website Field in Photographer Profile
issues.main_description_html:
Description

Feedback was raised about the Website field on the Photographer Profile (Photographer Directory).

A photographer on a Basic account (example: Mark de Graaf, username panomark, markdegraaf360@gmail.com) has a website displayed on his public 360photographers.org profile (panomark-360.com). However, in the GoThru dashboard Photographer Profile → Website (Link) field, the input is locked and shows "Upgrade to Pro/Agency to add a website".

This creates two problems:

  1. He already has a website value showing publicly, but can no longer change it because the field is gated behind a Pro/Agency upgrade.
  2. His website has since changed (now https://panomark.nl), so the public directory is displaying a stale, uneditable URL.

Reporter's view: Basic accounts should be allowed to have/edit the website field, since they are paying customers.

Requirement

  • Allow Basic account photographers to add and edit the Website (Link) field on their Photographer Profile.
  • Remove / adjust the "Upgrade to Pro/Agency to add a website" gate so it no longer blocks Basic users from editing this field.
  • Ensure the website entered in the dashboard correctly reflects on the public 360photographers.org profile.
  • Update text into "Upgrade your plan to add a website" for Pay as You Go status
  • Update text into "Upgrade your plan to edit a website" for if you previously added a link while on the Basic/Pro/Agency plan and then reverted to the Pay as You Go status (you cannot add or edit links)

Acceptance Criteria

  1. A Basic-account photographer can enter and save a website URL in the Photographer Profile Website (Link) field.
  2. The "Upgrade to Pro/Agency to add a website" lock no longer appears for Basic accounts (or is updated per the agreed access policy).
  3. The saved website value is editable at any time and is correctly displayed on the public photographer directory profile.
  4. Existing photographers who already have a website value (but were locked out) can now update it are enable if status Basic/Pro/Agency.
  5. Pay as You Go Status can't add or edit a website

Reference: docs/superpowers/specs/analyze-all.md — see §3 Persona A (Photographer), §4 (Photographer profile & directory), and §5.1 (Subscription tiers: Basic vs. Pro/Agency).

Demo by Developer
